phrases.french: Un billet pour Paris, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.phonetic: un bee-YEH poor pah-REE, seel voo PLAY
phrases.context: Reims is 45 minutes from Paris by TGV (high-speed train). The TGV station is Reims Champagne-Ardenne, which is separate from the central Reims station — confirm which station your train uses.

phrases.french: Est-ce que ce train s'arrête à Reims Champagne-Ardenne ?
phrases.english: Does this train stop at Reims Champagne-Ardenne?
phrases.phonetic: es-kuh suh TRAN sah-RET ah RANS shom-PAH-nyuh ar-DEN
phrases.context: Reims has two stations: the central city station and the out-of-town TGV station (Reims Champagne-Ardenne). Most Paris TGVs use the out-of-town station — check before boarding.

tips: Paris is just 45 minutes by TGV — Reims makes an excellent day trip from Paris
Watch out: Reims has TWO stations. TGVs use Reims Champagne-Ardenne (out of town), local trains use central Reims
Always validate (composter) your ticket before boarding — yellow machines on the platform
